message.content:
Here’s a message that should work in both a casual startup and a conservative corporate environment. I’ll assume:
- You need a few days off (not emergency same-day).
- You’re willing to say it’s a personal matter but not give details.
- Email or chat message is acceptable.

---

Subject: Request for Time Off

Hi [Manager’s Name],

I hope you’re doing well. I’m writing to request time off from [start date] through [end date] due to a personal matter that I need to attend to. I’d prefer not to go into details, but it is important and I appreciate your understanding.

I will make sure that my current tasks are up to date before I’m away and will hand off anything time-sensitive to the appropriate team members. I’m also happy to help with any transition or coverage planning you think is needed before I’m out.

Please let me know if these dates are workable or if we need to adjust them.

Best regards,  
[Your Name]

---

You can shorten this for chat (e.g., Slack/Teams) by dropping the subject line and slightly tightening the wording.
